summary: Indian markets have been volatile and many investors are worried because recent returns feel lower than FD. In this video, we explain what is really happening in the market, why equity should be viewed with a 3–5 year horizon, and how to invest with discipline instead of chasing what is trending (silver, smallcaps, etc.).

chapters.label: Why 1 year returns can mislead (FD vs equity)
Avoid chasing trends: silver vs smallcaps
SIP during downturns: what to do

seo.title: Markets Are Flat. Don’t Panic.The Real Wealth Game Is Patience.

takeaways.text: Judging equity on one year of returns and holding it against an FD can mislead you, because equity is built to be measured over a 3 to 5 year horizon, not a few volatile months.
Stopping your SIP when the market is falling can quietly hurt your long-term outcome, so the discipline matters most exactly when it feels hardest to keep.
Chasing whatever is trending, silver or smallcaps, is a different game from investing with a plan, and the episode sides with discipline over the trend.
A lump sum does not have to go in all at once: a staggered approach lets you deploy it in steps instead of betting everything on one moment.
Geopolitics, FII flows and INR moves all push markets around, which is why asset allocation and rebalancing are framed as ways to lower risk rather than predict the next turn.
